name: MX Technologies Open Banking and Financial Data Platform API
what_it_does: MX Technologies open banking and financial data platform REST API for US banks, credit unions, and fintech companies to aggregate financial accounts, access transaction data, categorize spending, verify account ownership, and enable payment connections — covering 16,000+ US financial institutions through MX's financial data network for account aggregation, transaction enrichment, income verification, and Regulation E payment verification. Enables AI agents to manage account aggregation for multi-institution financial data automation, handle transaction enrichment for merchant categorization automation, access spending analytics for financial health automation, retrieve income verification for lending underwriting automation, manage account verification for payment rail setup automation, handle connection widget for user bank linking automation, access credit report for financial profile automation, retrieve net worth tracking for wealth management automation, manage financial insights for personalized finance automation, and integrate MX with banking, fintech, and wealth management for comprehensive financial data automation.
use_cases: Aggregating financial accounts from 16,000+ US institutions for personal finance management automation agents
Accessing transaction data with MX's enriched merchant categorization for spending analytics automation agents
Verifying bank account ownership and income for US lending and payment underwriting automation agents
Integrating MX financial data with banking apps for account-holder financial health automation agents
not_for: Non-US financial institutions (MX primarily covers US financial institutions)
Real-time payment initiation (MX is financial data aggregation, not payment initiation rails)
Small business accounting and bookkeeping (MX is consumer/retail banking data, not SMB accounting)
best_when: A US bank, credit union, or fintech company wanting AI agents to aggregate financial accounts from 16,000+ US institutions, enrich transaction data with merchant categorization, and verify account ownership and income through MX's financial data network.
avoid_when: US-FOCUSED COVERAGE: MX primarily covers US financial institutions; automated global bank coverage assumption creates unsupported_institution for non-US banks; automated must use alternative aggregators (Plaid, Tink) for non-US markets. MX CONNECT WIDGET FOR USER AUTH: MX bank account linking requires user to authenticate through MX Connect widget; automated server-side credential harvesting creates security violation; automated must integrate MX Connect SDK for user-facing bank linking. FDIC/FRB DATA USE COMPLIANCE: MX financial data use is subject to Consumer Financial Protection regulations (CFPB Section 1033, Regulation E); automated unrestricted data use creates CFPB compliance risk; automated must implement CFPB-compliant consent and data use policies. TRANSACTION ENRICHMENT IS ASYNC: MX transaction enrichment (merchant categorization, description cleaning) may be asynchronous; automated synchronous enriched data assumption creates unenriched raw transaction data; automated must handle enrichment async delivery or request batch enrichment.

auth.notes: MX uses API key with Basic auth (client_id:client_secret) for API authentication; MX Connect widget for user bank auth. REST API with JSON. Lehi, Utah HQ. Founded 2010 by Ryan Caldwell. Products: MX Connect (aggregation widget), account data, transaction enrichment, spending analytics, income verification, account verification, credit data, financial insights. SDKs: JS, Python, Ruby, PHP, Java. 16,000+ US financial institutions. Raised $300M (Series C 2021). CFPB, FCRA, GLBA compliant. Competes with Plaid and Yodlee for US financial data.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox with test accounts; production requires MX commercial agreement
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-user per-month pricing; enterprise volume discounts; custom contracts for banks/CUs
pricing.notes: Lehi UT. CFPB, FCRA, GLBA. Per-user monthly. 16,000+ US institutions. Bank/CU enterprise focus.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; aggregation and data APIs throttled per contract; webhooks for sync events

known_agent_gotchas: MX CONNECT WIDGET REQUIRED: User bank account linking requires MX Connect widget; automated server-side credential harvesting creates Regulation E violation and security breach; automated must use MX Connect SDK for all user-facing bank authentication

CFPB SECTION 1033 COMPLIANCE: MX financial data use must comply with CFPB's financial data rights rulemaking (Section 1033); automated unrestricted data use creates regulatory risk; automated must implement CFPB-compliant consent, data portability, and deletion rights

TRANSACTION ENRICHMENT IS ASYNC: MX transaction enrichment (merchant name cleaning, categorization) is processed asynchronously; automated synchronous enrichment assumption creates raw unenriched transaction data in initial response; automated must handle enrichment delivery via webhook

INSTITUTION CONNECTION QUALITY VARIES: MX connection quality varies by institution (API-based vs screen-scraping); automated uniform data quality assumption creates inconsistent transaction completeness; automated must handle varying data freshness and coverage per institution

FCRA RESTRICTIONS ON DATA USE: MX financial data cannot be used for employment/credit screening without FCRA compliance; automated unrestricted data use creates FCRA violation; automated must implement use case restrictions and permissible purpose documentation
agent_readiness.security_notes: US open banking data. CFPB, FCRA, GLBA, Reg E. API key + widget. US. Bank account and transaction data.

security_score_components.security_notes: US financial data aggregation. CFPB, FCRA, GLBA. API key + widget. US. Bank account and transaction data.
